content: When building language models or autonomous agents that leverage the Reflexion framework for iterative self-improvement, we can introduce partial_reflexion testing that only activates self-reflection on a sampled subset of model inferences, rather than forcing self-reflection after every generated output. This approach trades off a small reduction in average performance per inference for large gains in overall compute efficiency, and can reveal heterogeneous patterns in when self-reflection actually improves output versus when it introduces unproductive overcorrection.
